summary: GVE Level 2 Document Authority defines the normative cross-document authority graph for the Level 2 specification set. It establishes explicit same-level imports, transitive visibility, deterministic identifier resolution, relationship-endpoint visibility, import acyclicity, global identifier uniqueness, and fail-closed conflict behavior. It preserves the Level 2 root decomposition and distinguishes specification-document visibility from workflow dependencies and runtime data handoffs.

definitions.text: The directed graph whose nodes are normative Level 2 specification documents and whose edges are explicit document imports. The graph governs authority visibility only and does not represent workflow execution order or runtime data flow.
An explicit same-level visibility edge from one Level 2 document to another Level 2 document named in the importing document's document.imports array.
A definition, requirement, or relationship identifier declared directly by the document being validated.
The importing document together with every document reachable by following zero or more explicit import edges.
An identifier declared locally or by a document in the importing document's transitive import closure.
The process of resolving a reference to exactly one globally unique visible identifier without precedence, shadowing, implicit imports, or search-order dependence.

Any duplicate specification identity, duplicate semantic identifier, ambiguous ownership, invalid parentage, unresolved root, unresolved import, import cycle, unresolved visible reference, or unresolved relationship endpoint.
The requirement that each relationship source and target resolve to a visible definition or requirement in the declaring document's import closure.

The normative separation between document imports, which grant specification identifier visibility, and workflow dependencies or runtime handoffs, which govern execution or data transfer and are outside this document's authority.

requirements.text: Every normative Level 2 subordinate document must declare document role subordinate, specification-set root GVE-LEVEL-2, and specification parent GVE-LEVEL-2.

Every Level 2 document import must be explicit, must name an existing normative Level 2 document, and must not be inferred from parentage, filenames, references, workflow dependencies, handoffs, or repository traversal.
A Level 2 document must not import itself or any document outside numeric Level 2.
The Level 2 import graph must be acyclic. Any direct or transitive import cycle must fail validation.
A reference declared by a Level 2 document may resolve only to a local identifier or to an identifier declared by a document in its transitive import closure.
Identifier resolution must be deterministic and must not use precedence, shadowing, fallback search, implicit imports, declaration order, filesystem order, or validator implementation order.
Specification identities and all definition, requirement, and relationship identifiers must be globally unique across the complete governed specification set. Any duplicate must fail validation.

Every relationship source and target must resolve to a visible definition or requirement. A relationship must not target a relationship identifier or an identifier outside the declaring document's import closure.
An unresolved specification-set root, invalid subordinate parent, unresolved import, unresolved requirement reference, or unresolved relationship endpoint must fail closed.

Conflicting subordinate documents must fail closed. No document may override, shadow, supersede, or gain precedence over another Level 2 document through import order, path order, naming, or declaration order.

Specification-document imports grant authority visibility only. They must not create workflow execution ordering, operation dependencies, runtime data transfer, handoff satisfaction, or plugin-specific payload meaning.

Validation of the complete governed specification set must evaluate the full document graph before accepting cross-document resolution and must produce the same result independently of document discovery order.

includes: Normative Level 2 subordinate-document role, root, and parentage rules
Deterministic local and imported identifier resolution
Global specification and semantic identifier uniqueness
Fail-closed unresolved and conflicting document behavior
The distinction between specification imports and runtime dependencies or handoffs
excludes: Workflow execution ordering and operation-plan acceptance
Runtime data transfer and handoff satisfaction
Precedence, shadowing, fallback resolution, and implicit imports
